definition: A binding factor that ties a person to cyclical becoming and obstructs full release.
notes: The project keeps fetter as the default because it remains the clearest concise term in English for the canonical set. Bond can be useful in explanatory prose when the binding function is more important than the traditional doctrinal label.

context_rules.context: In lists and doctrinal discussions of the ten fetters
In explanatory prose emphasizing the binding function
context_rules.notes: Acceptable alternate if the traditional label is not needed.

example_phrases.notes: General plural reference to the standard doctrinal set.
Shows a common sub-classification of the fetters.
